title: Tropical Ideals
speaker: Felipe Rincón
abstract:
Tropical ideals are combinatorial objects that abstract the behavior of the collections of subsets of lattice points that arise as the supports of all polynomials in an ideal. Their structure is governed by a sequence of ‘compatible’ matroids and, even though most tropical ideals are not realizable by an ideal of polynomials, they share many properties with usual ideals in a polynomial ring.
In this talk, I will introduce the notion of tropical ideals and discuss various works studying some of their properties and their possible associated (tropical) varieties. I will also share some results about the class of matroids that can be represented as the variety of a tropical ideal, and some recent developments in the study of a tropical Nullstellensatz.

proof_stages.label: Support-based tropicalization of ideals
proof_stages.summary: The talk first recalls tropicalization by replacing a classical ideal with the collection of supports of its polynomials and defining the tropical variety by the max-attained-twice condition, producing balanced polyhedral fans that retain data such as dimension and degree.

proof_stages.label: Axioms and matroid-tower interpretation of tropical ideals
proof_stages.summary: Motivated by tropical schemes, the speaker axiomatizes collections of supports by emptiness, monomial-shift closure, union closure, and elimination, then reformulates a tropical ideal as a compatible infinite tower of matroids on bounded-degree monomials.

proof_stages.label: Realizability and a nonrealizable tropical ideal
proof_stages.summary: After distinguishing realizable tropical ideals from arbitrary axiomatic ones, the speaker constructs a two-variable tropical ideal from circuits lying in standard triangles and proves it cannot arise from any classical ideal by forcing a polynomial product whose support is not allowed.

proof_stages.label: Hilbert functions, varieties, and balancing
proof_stages.summary: The matroid ranks in each degree define a Hilbert function that eventually agrees with a polynomial, the associated tropical variety is a finite polyhedral complex of the predicted dimension, and initial-degeneration multiplicities make it balanced, while notions such as prime and irreducible remain unclear.

proof_stages.label: Tropical realizability of matroids and tensor-product obstructions
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines a matroid to be tropically realizable when its Bergman fan is the variety of a tropical ideal, shows linear realizability implies this notion, and explains the obstruction proving that the Vámos matroid direct-summed with U_{2,3} is not tropically realizable via nonexistence of a required matroid tensor product; the class is minor-closed and has infinitely many forbidden minors.

proof_stages.label: Tropical Nullstellensatz with saturation
proof_stages.summary: A tropical Nullstellensatz is stated: containment of the variety of a tropical ideal in a tropical hypersurface forces, at least after lattice-point saturation of a sufficiently high Minkowski power of the support set, membership in the ideal, with sharper realizable cases and consequences for empty and zero-dimensional varieties.

proof_stages.label: Finite description, operations, and applications agenda
proof_stages.summary: The final part lays out open problems: how to finitely describe useful subclasses despite uncountably many one-variable tropical ideals, how to define generated ideals and algebraic operations such as products and intersections, what extra classical geometric information tropical ideals capture beyond tropical varieties, and how matroid invariants behave asymptotically along the tower.
